mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and lower discount rates, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the easing trend is sustained.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS than during flat or rising FEDFUNDS periods, or if the sign flips in a rolling 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS move above 4.5% marks a tightening regime that raises hurdle rates and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days once the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if post-crossing 20-day SPX returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or if the thresholded signal has negative t-statistics in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth identifies weakening labor income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp while PAYEMS 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the combined condition does not produce lower-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone explains the effect as well as the joint rule.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS indicates improving nominal consumer spending and revenue momentum, which supports near-term earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 1- and 2-month changes in RSAFS do not align with above-average 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign is unstable across successive quarterly vintages.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in INDPRO signals output and production weakness that precedes lower sales growth and weaker equity performance, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month declines in INDPRO fail to predict lower 60-day SPX returns relative to expanding production periods, or if the effect disappears after accounting for revision risk.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below recent trend marks housing-demand contraction that transmits to broader cyclical activity and weakens S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the specified housing drawdown does not precede below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if drawdowns are followed by positive returns with no lagged weakness.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and RSAFS is also rising, the joint signal identifies synchronized real activity and nominal sales expansion that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-RSAFS condition does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not statistically distinguishable from noise.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, low and stable UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS gains sustains consumer confidence and earnings resilience, which keeps S&P 500 returns positive over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE at or below 4.5% and PAYEMS 3-month change positive
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% and PAYEMS at 159398 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the low-unemployment plus payroll-growth condition fails to produce above-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if returns turn negative despite the trigger holding in repeated samples.
